title: #[non_exhaustive] on SieveErrorKind and SieveAction is correct library discipline
description: Both SieveErrorKind and SieveAction are marked #[non_exhaustive]. A reviewer might suggest removing the annotation to avoid requiring callers to write a catch-all '_' arm in match expressions. That is incorrect advice. As a published library, adding new SieveAction variants (e.g. Redirect, Vacation) or new SieveErrorKind variants is a normal extension path. Without #[non_exhaustive], every such addition would be a semver-breaking change requiring a major version bump. The annotation is the correct forward-compatibility mechanism. Do not remove it.
